ssl/MemoryBIO/eof.name: eof
ssl/MemoryBIO/eof.description: A boolean indicating whether the memory BIO is current at the end-of-file position.

info.excerpt: A boolean indicating whether the memory BIO is current at the end-of-file position.

ssl/enum_certificates.name: enum_certificates
ssl/enum_certificates.description: Retrieve certificates from Windows’ system cert store. store_name may be one of CA, ROOT or MY. Windows may provide additional cert stores, too. The function returns a list of (cert_bytes, encoding_type, trust) tuples. The encoding_type specifies the encoding of cert_bytes. It is either x509_asn for X.509 ASN.1 data or pkcs_7_asn for PKCS#7 ASN.1 data. Trust specifies the purpose of the certificate as a set of OIDS or exactly True if the certificate is trustworthy for all purposes. Example: >...

info.excerpt: Retrieve certificates from Windows’ system cert store. store_name may be one of CA, ROOT or MY. Windows may provide additional cert stores, too. The function returns a list of (cert_bytes, encoding_ty...

ssl/enum_crls.name: enum_crls
ssl/enum_crls.description: Retrieve CRLs from Windows’ system cert store. store_name may be one of CA, ROOT or MY. Windows may provide additional cert stores, too. The function returns a list of (cert_bytes, encoding_type, trust) tuples. The encoding_type specifies the encoding of cert_bytes. It is either x509_asn for X.509 ASN.1 data or pkcs_7_asn for PKCS#7 ASN.1 data. Availability: Windows. Added in version 3.4.
